title: Germany vs. Paraguay - More Markets
description: More markets for the FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for June 29 at 4:30 PM ET.

markets.question: Germany vs. Paraguay: Germany O/U 2.5
markets.description: In the upcoming FIFA World Cup game between Germany and Paraguay, scheduled for June 29 at 4:30 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if Germany score 3 or more goals within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. If Germany score less than 3 goals within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time, this market will resolve to "Under". This market is based solely on Germany's score, not the combined score of both teams. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. All markets will settle based on the official final result as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. Revisions to officially declared final scores made after market resolution will not be accounted for in determining the outcome.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany enters the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 32 matchup against Paraguay as the clear market favorite, buoyed by topping Group E despite a late 2-1 loss to Ecuador and boasting superior depth, attacking options such as Leroy Sané and Florian Wirtz, and experience from multiple prior tournaments. Paraguay advanced via a scoreless draw with Australia that underscored their defensive organization and counter-attacking threat, though they face a significant step up in quality against a German side that historically prevailed 1-0 in their only previous World Cup meeting. The June 29 contest at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough offers neutral-site conditions with both teams rested after group stage conclusions; recent roster stability and Paraguay’s clean-sheet form represent the primary variables that could influence implied probabilities in winner and total goals markets.
